initiativeCore.problemStatement: Build a single‑file browser‑based Sudoku game where a user can start a new puzzle, fill in numbers, check progress, and complete the board. The app must display a clean 9x9 grid with 3x3 sub‑boxes, allow cell selection, number entry (1‑9), clear mistakes, and differentiate fixed starting numbers from user‑entered numbers. Include three difficulty levels (easy, medium, hard) with valid puzzles that have a single solution. Prevent editing of original numbers, highlight selected row/column/box, optionally flag duplicate numbers or conflicts. Provide reset, new puzzle, timer, mistake counter, pause/resume, and a completion screen showing time, difficulty, and mistakes. Deliver a polished, responsive UI that works on desktop and mobile, supports keyboard input, and is fully offline packaged in one HTML file.
goals: Deliver a functional Sudoku game in a single HTML file
Support three difficulty levels with valid puzzles
Provide responsive UI for desktop and mobile
Include timer, mistake counter, pause/resume, and completion screen
Ensure no network requests; the game works completely offline
nonGoals: Implement a sophisticated puzzle generator algorithm
initiativeCore.businessImpact: If successful, the project demonstrates the team’s ability to build clean UI state, validation logic, and responsive design within strict constraints, serving as a portfolio piece and internal proof‑of‑concept. Failure would indicate gaps in front‑end engineering capability and could delay hiring assessments or product demos that rely on this showcase.
initiativeCore.stakeholders: Product Owner, Front‑end Development Team, QA Engineer, UX Designer, Internal Demo Audience

functional.description: Render a 9x9 Sudoku grid with clear 3x3 sub‑box borders
Allow the user to start a new puzzle selecting difficulty (easy, medium, hard)
Prevent editing of fixed starting numbers
Enable cell selection and number entry (1‑9) via keyboard or on‑screen controls
Highlight the selected row, column, and sub‑box
Optionally highlight duplicate numbers or obvious conflicts; this feature can be turned on or off by the user
Provide reset and new‑puzzle functionality while preserving selected difficulty
Detect puzzle completion and display a completion screen with time, difficulty, and mistake count
Include a timer that starts on first move, can be paused/resumed only when the app is manually paused (screen goes black and timer stops), and a mistake counter that increments on invalid moves
Ensure the game works on both desktop and mobile browsers with responsive layout
Provide a UI control that allows the user to enable or disable duplicate‑number highlighting

nonFunctional.description: Package all code, styles, and assets in a single HTML file
Operate fully offline with no network requests
Provide a polished UI with clear visual states for selected, fixed, invalid, and completed cells
Load instantly (under 1 second) on typical browsers
Support keyboard navigation and include appropriate ARIA labels for accessibility
Require JavaScript to be enabled in the browser for the game to function

edgeCases: User attempts to edit a fixed cell
User enters a number outside 1‑9 range
Puzzle is reset while timer is running
Multiple duplicate numbers in a row/column/box
Timer is paused and resumed rapidly
User loads the page on a browser with JavaScript disabled
User toggles duplicate‑highlight feature on or off

criteria.description: The Sudoku grid renders with 9 rows and 9 columns and visible 3x3 sub‑box borders
Selecting a cell highlights its row, column, and sub‑box
Fixed numbers are visually distinct and cannot be edited
User can input numbers 1‑9 via keyboard or UI; inputs outside this range are rejected
Timer starts on first move, pauses/resumes correctly only when manually paused (screen goes black), and stops on puzzle completion
Mistake counter increments when a user creates a conflict or enters an invalid number
A completion screen appears when the puzzle is solved, showing elapsed time, selected difficulty, and total mistakes
The game layout adapts to mobile screen sizes and remains fully playable
All resources load from the single HTML file; network tab shows no external requests
Reset and New Puzzle buttons work, preserving the chosen difficulty level
User can enable or disable duplicate‑number highlighting via the provided UI control, and the grid updates accordingly

measurableOutcomes: User can complete a puzzle of each difficulty within reasonable time
Mistake counter remains below a defined threshold for typical play
Responsive layout passes viewport width tests on common devices

factors: Single‑file constraint may limit future extensibility
Potential performance issues on low‑end devices
mitigations: Structure code modularly within the single file
Perform performance testing on a range of devices

deliveryIntent.rolloutExpectations: Internal stakeholder demo followed by optional public showcase on company website
deliveryIntent.timelineAssumptions: Assume 4 weeks of development effort including testing
deliveryIntent.budget: No external budget; effort covered by internal team resources
